index.description: L2 index of methodologies in the frontend domain. The retriever reads this after picking frontend from L1 domains.xml. Entries list slug + tier + summary; open the leaf AGENTS.md / content/*.xml for full payload.

methodology.summary: Per-screen accessibility audit checklist running 9 critical WCAG-AA checks (semantic landmarks, focus order, contrast, alt text, ARIA correctness, keyboard nav, error messaging, motion-respect, screen-reader labels); produces a per-screen pass/fail row set with named fixes.

Specification of accessibility expectations for a frontend codebase: WCAG-AA conformance target, tooling baseline, per-role responsibilities, and regression-prevention contract; produces an a11y spec that the team agrees to and that subsequent audits assert against.

Pattern for asserting accessibility invariants in code: unit-level component contracts, integration-level axe assertions, and CI gates that block on critical violations; produces a code scaffold + CI config making a11y regressions impossible to merge silently.
Three-pillar rubric (simplicity, naturalness, context-awareness) for auditing or designing voice-UI prompts: one idea per turn, no robotic phrasing, never restate what the user just said.
Pipeline spec for distributing design tokens from a single source (Tokens Studio / Figma) to CSS / SCSS / iOS Swift / Android XML / React Native using Style Dictionary, with CI-time drift detection.

Advanced CSS-in-JS patterns covering SSR critical-CSS extraction, atomic compile-time variants, theme runtime swapping, and zero-runtime alternatives; produces a code scaffold + decision matrix selecting library (styled-components, Linaria, vanilla-extract, Panda CSS) per constraint.

Baseline patterns for CSS-in-JS in component libraries: prop-driven styles, theme provider, scoped class names, and TypeScript-typed style props; produces a starter scaffold matching the chosen library with naming + file-layout conventions.
Four-pillar rubric (ownership / usable components / strong docs / measured adoption) to grade a design system's health and pinpoint the weakest pillar before more components ship.

Baseline design-tokens spec covering canonical categories (color, spacing, typography, radius, shadow, motion), naming convention, alias-vs-base layering, and single-source-of-truth contract; produces a tokens.json spec consumed by both design tools and code.

Implementation playbook for design tokens: tokens.json schema, style-dictionary (or equivalent) build pipeline, CI sync to platform outputs, and a Figma-to-code round-trip; produces a working tokens repo with build + lint + sync scripts.
Spec for XR (AR/VR/MR) apps in enterprise contexts: long-session comfort budget, tool-integration map, security review, accessibility compliance, ROI-measurable outcomes.
Three-step error-recovery ladder (rapid reprompt → escalated prompt → bailout / escalate to human) spec for voice UIs: defines per-step copy, retry budget, telemetry, and escape route.

Four-phase workflow exploring 3\u20135 distinct UI variants before committing to one: capture requirements, brainstorm via agent into designs/variant-N-slug/, user selects + refines, Storybook + component agents finalise; produces a design folder with N variants and a selected-variant manifest.

Mobile-first responsive design spec: breakpoint catalogue, container-query strategy, touch-target minimums, viewport-meta + safe-area handling, and per-component breakpoint policy; produces a spec the codebase asserts against in lint + tests.
Next.js App Router spec: route segments, layouts, loading/error boundaries, server vs client components, server actions, and data-fetch caching policy.
Advanced PWA spec: Web Push (VAPID subscribe/send), offline data sync (IndexedDB queue + Background Sync + retry), and update management (skipWaiting + user banner).
PWA core spec: Web App Manifest (stable id + maskable icons), origin-root Service Worker, controllerchange-driven update flow, and Workbox caching strategies per route shape.
React component architecture spec: feature-based module layout, colocation, compound components, data-vs-presentation split, and Suspense boundaries.

Spec for a semantic-token layer (`color.surface.brand`, `color.text.muted`) sitting between primitives and components, with mode definitions (light/dark/high-contrast/density) and Figma variable bindings.
SEO spec for JavaScript SPAs: SSR/SSG decision, structured-data scope, Core Web Vitals budgets, canonical/hreflang, and sitemap/robots wiring.
shadcn/ui spec: vendored copy-in components (npx shadcn add), Tailwind + Radix primitives, CVA variants, and per-component file ownership in components/ui/.
shadcn/ui architecture spec: vendored components/ui/ layer, CVA + cn() helpers, design-token bridge to Tailwind config, and the lift-path from ui/ → composed feature components.
Reference report of spatial-computing platforms (visionOS, Quest, HoloLens, Android XR), terminology (passthrough, anchoring, FOV, IPD), and market positioning to brief designers entering XR work.
Tool selection report and design-to-development workflow for spatial / XR design: Figma / Bezi / ShapesXR / Unity / Reality Composer Pro positioning, file handoff, and prototype-to-build path.

Spec for the five spatial input modalities (hand tracking / controllers / gaze / voice / gesture) × three interaction patterns (direct manipulation / ray-casting / gaze-and-dwell) with use cases, strengths, and accessibility implications.
Spec for panel anchoring (world / head / wrist), window management (single / tiled / stacked), and interaction rules for 3D / XR interfaces across visionOS / Meta Horizon / HoloLens / Android XR.
Storybook 8 + CSF 3 config spec: Meta<typeof Component>, autodocs tags, a11y + interactions addons, and Chromatic visual-regression baseline.
Tailwind CSS setup spec: tailwind.config.{ts,js} content paths, design-token theme extension, plugin set, and PurgeCSS-equivalent JIT discipline.
Tailwind production architecture spec: design-tokens-as-CSS-variables, theme layers, component-variant strategy, and the layer/component-class escalation ladder.
Tailwind variant-composition spec: cva() (class-variance-authority) for type-safe variants and cn() (clsx + tailwind-merge) for conflict-safe class merging.
Three-layer taxonomy spec (primitive → semantic → component-exception) plus strict `{category}.{role}.{state}` naming rules for design tokens so the system stays navigable past 500 tokens.
UI library foundation spec: directory layout, primitive set (button/input/modal), token bridge, Storybook stories, and CI gates (a11y + visual regression).
UI library advanced patterns spec: compound components, accessible modal portals, CSF 3 stories with interaction tests, and slot-based composition.

Pattern spec for designing voice UIs: prompt-writing rules, progressive error-recovery ladder, multimodal voice+screen coordination, platform constraints (Alexa / Google Actions / Siri), success metrics.
VUI for smart home and IoT: command-pattern taxonomy, multi-device coordination flows, partial-failure handling and undo support, emitted as a coherent VUI flow spec.
Privacy- and security-hardened VUI architecture: five privacy principles, split classifier/executor agents for PII, redaction policy and audit trail emitted as a privacy spec.
Generates a DTCG-compliant tokens.json with $type/$value/$description and {alias} references that round-trips between Figma, Style Dictionary v4, and native platforms.
W3C Design Tokens Community Group draft applied as a config artefact: $type/$value/$ref structure, token group taxonomy, validator-ready JSON conforming to the spec.
Generates a WCAG 2.2 AA conformance report for the 9 new success criteria (focus appearance, target size, drag, auth, redundant entry, consistent help) + automated CI gate.
